Rojohn is Technical Analyst (Technical Systems Analyst, IT Support & Automation) at Casto Travel Philippines, from April 2025 to present. He is based in San Pedro, Laguna (GSIS Village).

Rojohn has over 5 years of experience in technical support, systems administration, and automation. He has been with Casto Travel Philippines since December 2020 (first as IT Administrator, then as Technical Analyst from April 2025).
Rojohn has a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from Eulogio "Amang" Rodriguez Institute of Science and Technology (2016 – 2020).

Key projects include: (1) Organizational AI Bot — an internal AI chatbot for IT inquiries and workflows at Casto Travel Philippines; (2) HR Ticketing System (HRD Helpdesk) — internal HR portal; (3) Call Tracking Solution — web app for call and transaction tracking with Monday.com integration; (4) Company Website Redesign — modern Next.js corporate site. More details are in the Projects section of the portfolio.
You can reach Rojohn by email at rojohn1123@gmail.com or by phone at +63 921 794 2076. His LinkedIn profile is under Rojohn Michael De Guzman. A resume is available on the portfolio.

He uses Git, GitHub, Microsoft 365, APIs (Postman), Monday.com, MySQL, ManageEngine (Endpoint Central, ServiceDesk Plus), Python, Flask, PyQt5, LM Studio, SQLite, ChromaDB, MongoDB, JavaScript, React, Node.js, Express, PHP, Laravel, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and more. He has experience with both support tools (ticketing, remote desktop, Bitdefender) and development stacks.

Certifications include: Machine Learning using Python (Simplilearn/SkillUp); Introduction to ITIL® 4 Foundation (Simplilearn/SkillUp); Digital Security Fundamentals (Simplilearn/SkillUp); HP Customer Self-Repair Program and Basic Troubleshooting (Phil-Data Business Systems, Inc., Oct 2025); System Administration and IT Infrastructure Services (Google).

He focuses on: starting with the problem and the user's pain; keeping solutions simple and maintainable; documenting as he builds; shipping and iterating based on feedback; listening before building; automating repetitive tasks; testing with real use; and keeping security and clarity in mind.
